item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As other Pitch Haven works, I find this confusing but fascinating nonetheless. I think that my enjoyment of these pieces is somewhat marred by the fact that they require careful attention to detail and an acute observation of how they relate to one another and I read the skip list in numerical order so it may be days or months between one part of the story and another.

item.title: @Moonpaw
item.content:encoded: To answer your question about why Aggie seems to exist as both SCP-1913-1 and Agatha White at the same time, the current theory is that there's some kind of magic used by the Crafters (and possibly even the lower classes) that allows them to switch between two bodies. This would explain why Espin always carries around that stuffed bear in "No Safe Haven," and why Dr. Hayward and Agent Crowley are believed to be the reincarnations of Suwaird and Sari.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aw, now I feel really sad about Crowley. :(

+1 for you, and an "F" in the chat for Alva. (X)>

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Loving the Pitch Haven stuff so far. But I am confused on one point. Isn't Agatha 1913-1? So Agatha White, the Promethus Admin, is the same Agatha right? So did something happen to 1913, or is she just in 2 different places at once?

And you are still writing more for this hub right? I'd hate to see it end so soon!

item.title: FANTEM SENPAI YER BACK
item.content:encoded: ASDVNAKENFKCNADKSC HOW DIDN'T I SEE THIS SOONER. WOOOOOOOOOO +1 ALL THE WAY

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Woooot, glad I decided to check the Pitch Haven hub again otherwise I never would of found this. Still need to take the time to figure out a way to find pages by newest.
Welp time to actually go read this.
Edit: Finished reading it, and it's just as good as the rest of the series. Think I may need a guide/timeline when the series is done just because I have trouble keeping the characters from eden straight. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love pitch haven, and I think the SCP format and the way the entries are sprinkled through the lists work very well for the format of this IP. Two bunny ears and one broken claw up!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Loved it, Pitch Haven stuff always gets me. Keep it coming!! +

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ANormally, I'm against telling the vast majority of a story through interviews, but it works well here. Always happy to see more Pitch Haven as well and please pass along my compliments on the doll. It really boosts this story to have that pictured there.

item.title: Sounds of Hallelujahs and terrified shrieks
item.content:encoded: Glad to see Pitch Haven's back. We finally get to see Sarah in SCP form, and that is a terrifying picture without relying on gore. Nice Scip overall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Pitch Haven series always has such vivid, compelling imagery.

The plot continues to confuse the hell out of me, but I think I've got the bare bones of it straight in my head.

Also, I;m pretty sure the accompanying visuals have made me afraid of bunnies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Neat, I was wondering when you'd get around to Crowley.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fairly interesting addition to the Pitch Haven series, always glad to see more.

+1 all day any day

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno. My initial impressions are "I've seen this before" (the link to 1903 explains it, but I swear I've seen that exact second picture elsewhere) and "This doesn't stand on its own". Usually I'm way into Pitch Haven stuff, but this didn't grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You shouldn't have to read the other skips to find a story here. The story should be fairly clear.

And the whole PL thing is a part of the canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not having read anything else related to Pitch Haven, I'm uncertain to what degree my issues with this article are resolvable. Probably my biggest point of contention is the use and characterization of Prometheus Labs here. I'm unsure if this is a Pitch Haven thing, or an issue isolated to this skip, but I'm not a fan of it either way.

I also don't find this skip particularly interesting. Maybe there's a story here that I'm missing because I didn't read the crosslinks, but if the crosslinks are required to understand the skip, then it's not a very well constructed article.

The writing is decent, and the implications for the pitch haven canon/thing are pretty good as well. Overall, I thought it was an entertaining read, and the whole pitch haven canon/thing is pretty creative as well.

item.title: Author post
item.content:encoded:
The doll was created by Alana Geary, a good friend of mine.

Credit goes to photoutopia for the second picture.

Credit to the Euclid symbol on the pic on the front page of the tabs goes to SunnyClockwork

I'd just like to thank everyone who looked at this. Y'all were great.

Hope everyone enjoys!
